name: Clinical Practice Research Datalink
description: The Clinical Practice Research Datalink (CPRD) is a real-world research service supporting retrospective and prospective public health and clinical studies. It draws on anonymised primary care data from GP practices across the UK, with linkage to secondary care, disease registries, and mortality data. CPRD Aurum covers EMIS practices (primarily England) and CPRD GOLD covers Vision practices (UK-wide).
population_coverage: UK GP-registered population (current and historical records)

requirements.name: ISAC protocol approval
requirements.description: Submit a study protocol to the Independent Scientific Advisory Committee (ISAC) for scientific and governance review

requirements.name: CPRD licence agreement
requirements.description: Organisation must hold a valid CPRD data licence or enter into a data sharing agreement

requirements.name: Data specification
requirements.description: Detailed specification of the data extract required, including study population, variables, and linked datasets

access_steps.name: Prepare ISAC protocol
access_steps.description: Draft the study protocol including research question, study design, data requirements, and data minimisation justification

access_steps.name: Submit ISAC application
access_steps.description: Submit the protocol to ISAC for independent scientific and governance review

access_steps.name: ISAC review
access_steps.description: ISAC reviews the protocol for scientific merit, feasibility, and proportionality of data request

access_steps.name: Data licence and costing
access_steps.description: Agree data licence terms and costs with CPRD

access_steps.name: Data extraction and provision
access_steps.description: CPRD prepares and delivers the approved data extract to the research team

timeline.notes: Preparing ISAC protocol and supporting documentation
Data extraction, quality checks, and delivery

costs.notes: Academic studies typically GBP 2,000-10,000. Industry studies typically GBP 10,000-50,000+. Costs depend on dataset scope, linkages required, and study complexity.

strengths: Gold-standard primary care data with long patient histories
Data extraction available - researchers can work with data in their own environments
Excellent linkage to secondary care (HES), mortality, cancer, and mental health datasets
60M+ patient records (current and historical) across UK
Well-established with decades of research use and published validation studies
limitations: ISAC review can be slow, especially if revisions required
Costs are significant for industry-funded studies
Transitioning from GOLD (Vision) to Aurum (EMIS) - GOLD dataset shrinking
TRE access not yet available (planned for future)
Primary care focus - secondary care via linkage only
recent_changes: CPRD Aurum expanding as EMIS coverage grows
CPRD GOLD declining as practices migrate from Vision to EMIS
TRE capability in development to complement data extract model
